explanation.rule: The suffix -less is added to nouns to form adjectives meaning 'without' or 'lacking' something.

groups.note: Added to nouns to form adjectives meaning 'without' or 'lacking' a quality or thing

examples.definition: not helpful; having no practical purpose
not giving enough attention; likely to make mistakes
without a permanent place to live
unable to act or take care of oneself
with no chance of improvement or success

wordForms.definition: not helpful; having no practical value
wordForms.example: Without electricity, the device is completely useless.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to employ something for a purpose

wordForms.definition: not giving enough attention; likely to make mistakes
wordForms.example: A careless mistake cost the team the match.
wordForms.rootDefinition: attention given to avoid mistakes or danger

wordForms.definition: without a permanent place to live
wordForms.example: The charity provides meals for homeless people in the city.
wordForms.rootDefinition: the place where a person lives

wordForms.definition: unable to take care of oneself; without power to act
wordForms.example: The injured bird was helpless on the ground.

wordForms.definition: unable to relax or stay still; always wanting to move
wordForms.example: The children grew restless during the long car journey.
wordForms.rootDefinition: a period of relaxation or inactivity

wordForms.definition: having no end; continuing for a very long time
wordForms.example: The desert seemed to stretch in endless directions.
wordForms.rootDefinition: the final point or conclusion of something

wordForms.example: The spider looks scary but it is completely harmless.

wordForms.definition: not afraid of anything; very brave
wordForms.example: The fearless climber reached the summit in record time.
wordForms.rootDefinition: an unpleasant feeling caused by danger or threat

wordForms.definition: with no chance of improvement or success
wordForms.example: The situation seemed hopeless until help finally arrived.
wordForms.rootDefinition: a feeling that something good will happen

wordForms.definition: unable to speak because of surprise or strong emotion
wordForms.example: She was speechless when she heard the good news.
wordForms.rootDefinition: the ability to express thoughts using spoken words

multipleChoiceExercises.sentence: Without a map or phone signal, we felt completely ___ in the forest.
The spider looked frightening but turned out to be ___.
He made a ___ error by forgetting to save his work.
She was ___ when she received the surprise award on stage.
The meeting felt ___ — we kept going round in circles.
The old computer was so slow it was practically ___.

transformExercises.sentence: The volunteers help ___ people find shelter every winter. (HOME)
The audience was ___ after the incredible performance. (SPEECH)
He was so ___ during the flight that he walked up and down the aisle. (REST)
The soldiers showed ___ bravery throughout the battle. (FEAR)
It seemed ___ to keep trying after so many failures. (HOPE)
